headline: The homepage now shows the latest pipeline run as a retro terminal
summary: Add a retro terminal-styled widget to the homepage that turns the latest published run into a compact explore, score, build, test, and ship narrative using existing artifacts.

candidates.title: The homepage now shows the latest pipeline run as a retro terminal
candidates.summary: Add a retro terminal-styled widget to the homepage that renders the latest published run as a curated command-line summary using existing decision, feedback, test, and manifest artifacts.
keep: Makes the pipeline feel like something that actually ran, not just a set of static sections
Pays off the Command Garden name with visible command-line output
Reuses existing artifacts, so it adds no new backend or data-fetching cost
mustChange: Hide the section entirely when decision or winner data is missing
Normalize mixed historical test-results shapes so the widget works across shipped days
risks: A dark terminal treatment could clash with the lighter homepage palette if the styling is too stark
feedbackReferences: Visitors asked for an artistic command interface on the home screen
The homepage needed a clearer picture of what the latest pipeline run actually did

candidates.title: Recent Run Diff Snapshot
candidates.summary: Show a compact before-and-after diff summarizing what changed between the latest shipped day and the previous one.
keep: Makes daily progress easier to compare at a glance
mustChange: Needs a clear comparison model for artifacts that do not exist on both days
risks: Can read as an internal changelog instead of a user-facing homepage feature

candidates.title: Archive Filter Chips by Feature Type
candidates.summary: Add filter chips to the archive so visitors can browse UI, content, and infrastructure days separately.
keep: Would improve archive browsing once there are more shipped days
mustChange: Needs consistent tags across older manifest entries before it becomes reliable
risks: Solves a problem the site does not quite have yet with only a few shipped days

winner.title: The homepage now shows the latest pipeline run as a retro terminal
winner.summary: Add a retro terminal-styled widget to the homepage that turns the latest published run into a compact explore, score, build, test, and ship narrative using existing artifacts.

rationale: The terminal widget won because it makes the pipeline feel active without adding any new backend cost. It strengthens the Command Garden brand, turns existing artifacts into a legible narrative, and creates a screenshot-friendly focal point for the homepage while staying safely inside a single implementation cycle.

feedbackInfluence.summary: Homepage feedback asked for an artistic command interface instead of only cards and prose.
Visitors wanted the latest run to feel visible as a process, not just a completed outcome.
